title: Indonesian Officials Vow Again to Stabilize Rupiah, Draw Inflows
description: Indonesia’s finance and central bank officials said they will step up efforts to stabilize the currency and attract inflows after a week where the nation’s stocks tumbled at the fastest pace worldwide and its currency sank to all-time lows.

title: Second US Screwworm Case Confirmed in Texas by USDA
description: A second case of the deadly New World screwworm parasite has been confirmed in a Texas calf, heightening fears of a broader outbreak that could impact the US beef business.

title: US Drops $300 Million Fraud Case That Spotlighted Brian Kahn
description: US prosecutors dropped a $300 million fraud indictment against a former executive at Prophecy Asset Management accused of working with Brian Kahn, the disgraced founder of Franchise Group Inc., to cheat investors in their hedge fund.

title: Laboring Under Delhi’s Harsh Heat, Workers Must Choose Health or Wages
description: Severe heat waves have been hitting India since April, forcing many of the country’s essential workers to make tough decisions.

title: Mali Conflict Escalates as Rebel Alliance Seizes Key Cities
description: Jihadists have nearly tripled their attacks on urban areas. Analysts fear an extended standoff could lead to many more civilian deaths.

title: A President, His Prime Minister and the Bitter Rift Dividing Senegal
description: The two men became president and prime minister by defeating the political old guard in Senegal. Now they are fighting each other.

title: Iran’s Soccer Team Allowed Into U.S. for World Cup, but Many Staff Denied
description: After months of doubts over their participation, Iran’s players have received visas for the United States just days before the World Cup begins.

title: Armenians Vote Under Russia’s Shadow
description: Trump and Putin are backing different players in a contested country.

title: Arrests of critics in Ghana provokes alarm over free speech under Mahama
description: Ghana’s 14 arrests in 16 months linked to false news have raised concerns about free speech.

title: Ronaldo joins Portugal training ahead of sixth World Cup appearance
description: Portuguese star Cristiano Ronaldo hit the training pitch as he readies for his sixth World Cup appearance.

title: Gaza fishermen stay afloat with dinghies made from doorframes
description: Fishermen in Gaza are using dinghies made of reclaimed fiberglass, wood, and doorframes salvaged from rubble.

title: Iran war live: Iranian missiles target Kuwait, Bahrain after US-Iran clash
description: The United Nations reports that 1.4 million people are in need of aid in Lebanon amid Israel's attacks on the country.

title: Chinese carriers seize two-thirds share of post-pandemic air routes
description: Chinese carriers have captured an expanding slice of the international air travel market for routes connecting Chinese and overseas cities since the Covid-19 pandemic, according to data presented at an industry summit in Beijing on Friday. Local carriers currently held a 66.5 per cent market share compared with 33.5 per cent for foreign airlines, said IBA Group, a UK-based aviation intelligence company, which organised the event. Pre-pandemic, it was a 50:50 split between domestic and foreign...

title: Targeting US$610 billion longevity market, biotech firms race to reverse ageing
description: For Beijing-based METiS TechBio CEO Lai Tsai-ta, ageing is comparable to bugs building up in a complex software system – it happens once errors begin to accumulate in the genetic code of human cells, such as their DNA sequences. “Those errors can be reprogrammed. It becomes possible to use AI to read, rewrite and reverse cells, or at least slow the ageing process,” Lai said in an interview with the South China Morning Post. The process could begin by fixing “immune cells, such as T cells, as...

title: Raizen Inks $13 Billion Out-of-Court Debt Deal With Creditors
description: Raizen SA reached an out-of-court restructuring agreement with the majority of its creditors, marking a key step in the Brazilian sugar-and-ethanol producer’s efforts to rework its debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: US plans to fight flesh-eating screwworm outbreak with flies and dogs
description: The insect, which has been found in America for the first time since 1966, is a growing concern in the southern US.

title: A Sherpa Survived 6 Days Alone on Everest. His Family Says He Was Abandoned.
description: Dawa Sherpa, 57, was found alive on Thursday, nearly a week after he was last seen on the mountain. His wife says more could have been done to find him sooner.

title: Elon Musk Dominates List of Highest Paid C.E.O.s
description: Executive compensation is accelerating while rank-and-file workers lag, widening the pay gap in corporate America.
